articles.title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
articles.description:
After witnessing the strength of young patients on the course in 2014, he is taking on Boston again to help fund pediatric cancer care and research.

The post He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Andrew Mahr is running the Boston Marathon to support pediatric cancer care at MGH.
Mahr was inspired by young patients he met during the 2014 marathon.
Funds raised will directly benefit families and children receiving treatment at MGH.

articles.title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
articles.description:
A recent analysis estimates a tax cut would reduce state revenue by roughly $5.1 billion per year, while saving the median Massachusetts household around $1,250 annually. 

The post ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Proposed income tax cut could save median Massachusetts household $1,250 annually.
Tax cut may reduce state revenue by approximately $5.1 billion each year.
Residents express support for tax relief, emphasizing financial benefits for local families.

articles.title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
articles.description:
Months before her boyfriend died from ethylene glycol poisoning, Judy Church allegedly told a family friend, “If he ever cheats on me, I’ll poison him.”

The post A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Judy Church allegedly threatened to poison her boyfriend if he cheated.
Testimony in the trial began on March 20, 2026.
The case raises concerns about domestic violence in the Boston area.
